book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
On Repentance and Remission of Sins, and Concerning the Adversary.

ccel.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

title: On Repentance and Remission of Sins, and Concerning the Adversary.

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Ezekiel xviii.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The righteousness of the righteous shall be upon him, and the wickedness of the wicked shall be upon him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But if the wicked will turn from all his sins, &c.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A fearful thing is sin, and the sorest disease of the soul is transgression, secretly cutting its sinews, and becoming also the cause of eternal fire; an evil of a man’s own choosing, an offspring of the will.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For that we sin of our own free will the Prophet says plainly in a certain place: Yet I planted thee a fruitful vine, wholly true: how art thou turned to bitterness, (and become) the strange vine?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The planting was good, the fruit coming from the will is evil; and therefore the planter is blameless, but the vine shall be burnt with fire since it was planted for good, and bore fruit unto evil of its own will.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For God, according to the Preacher, made man upright, and they have themselves sought out many inventions.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For we are His workmanship, says the Apostle, created unto good works, which God afore prepared, that we should walk in them.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: So then the Creator, being good, created for good works; but the creature turned of its own free will to wickedness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Sin then is, as we have said, a fearful evil, but not incurable; fearful for him who clings to it, but easy of cure for him who by repentance puts it from him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For suppose that a man is holding fire in his hand; as long as he holds fast the live coal he is sure to be burned, but should he put away the coal, he would have cast away the flame also with it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If however any one thinks that he is not being burned when sinning, to him the Scripture saith, Shall a man wrap up fire in his bosom, and not burn his clothes?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For sin burns the sinews of the soul, [and breaks the spiritual bones of the mind, and darkens the light of the heart].

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But some one will say, What can sin be?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is it a living thing?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is it an angel?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is it a demon?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What is this which works within us?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It is not an enemy, O man, that assails thee from without, but an evil shoot growing up out of thyself.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Look right on with thine eyes, and there is no lust.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: [Keep thine own, and] seize not the things of others, and robbery has ceased.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Remember the Judgment, and neither fornication, nor adultery, nor murder, nor any transgression of the law shall prevail with thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But whenever thou forgettest God, forthwith thou beginnest to devise wickedness and to commit iniquity.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Yet thou art not the sole author of the evil, but there is also another most wicked prompter, the devil.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He indeed suggests, but does not get the mastery by force over those who do not consent.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Therefore saith the Preacher, If the spirit of him that hath power rise up against thee, quit not thy place.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Shut thy door, and put him far from thee, and he shall not hurt thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But if thou indifferently admit the thought of lust, it strikes root in thee by its suggestions, and enthrals thy mind, and drags thee down into a pit of evils.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But perhaps thou sayest, I am a believer, and lust does not gain the ascendant over me, even if I think upon it frequently.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Knowest thou not that a root breaks even a rock by long persistence?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Admit not the seed, since it will rend thy faith asunder: tear out the evil by the root before it blossom, lest from being careless at the beginning thou have afterwards to seek for axes and fire.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When thine eyes begin to be diseased, get them cured in good time, lest thou become blind, and then have to seek the physician.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The devil then is the first author of sin, and the father of the wicked: and this is the Lord’s saying, not mine, that the devil sinneth from the beginning: none sinned before him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But he sinned, not as having received necessarily from nature the propensity to sin, since then the cause of sin is traced back again to Him that made him so; but having been created good, he has of his own free will become a devil, and received that name from his action.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For being an Archangel he was afterwards called a devil from his slandering: from being a good servant of God he has become rightly named Satan; for “Satan” is interpreted the adversary.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And this is not my teaching, but that of the inspired prophet Ezekiel: for he takes up a lamentation over him and says, Thou wast a seal of likeness, and a crown of beauty; in the Paradise of God wast thou born: and soon after, Thou wast born blameless in thy days, from the day in which thou wast created, until thine iniquities were found in thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Very rightly hath he said, were found in thee; for they were not brought in from without, but thou didst thyself beget the evil.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The cause also he mentions forthwith: Thine heart was lifted up because of thy beauty: for the multitude of thy sins wast thou wounded, and I did cast thee to the ground.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In agreement with this the Lord says again in the Gospels: I beheld Satan as lightning fall from heaven.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest the harmony of the Old Testament with the New.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He when cast out drew many away with him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It is he that puts lusts into them that listen to him: from him come adultery, fornication, and every kind of evil.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Through him our forefather Adam was cast out for disobedience, and exchanged a Paradise bringing forth wondrous fruits of its own accord for the ground which bringeth forth thorns.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What then? some one will say.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We have been beguiled and are lost.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is there then no salvation left?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We have fallen: Is it not possible to rise again?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We have been blinded: May we not recover our sight?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We have become crippled: Can we never walk upright?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In a word, we are dead: May we not rise again?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He that woke Lazarus who was four days dead and already stank, shall He not, O man, much more easily raise thee who art alive?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He who shed His precious blood for us, shall Himself deliver us from sin.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us not despair of ourselves, brethren; let us not abandon ourselves to a hopeless condition.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For it is a fearful thing not to believe in a hope of repentance.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For he that looks not for salvation spares not to add evil to evil: but to him that hopes for cure, it is henceforth easy to be careful over himself.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The robber who looks not for pardon grows desperate; but, if he hopes for forgiveness, often comes to repentance.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What then, does the serpent cast its slough, and shall not we cast off our sin?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thorny ground also, if cultivated well, is turned into fruitful; and is salvation to us irrecoverable?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nay rather, our nature admits of salvation, but the will also is required.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: God is loving to man, and loving in no small measure.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For say not, I have committed fornication and adultery: I have done dreadful things, and not once only, but often: will He forgive?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Will He grant pardon?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear what the Psalmist says: How great is the multitude of Thy goodness, O Lord!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thine accumulated offences surpass not the multitude of God’s mercies: thy wounds surpass not the great Physician’s skill.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Only give thyself up in faith: tell the Physician thine ailment: say thou also, like David: I said, I will confess me my sin unto the Lord: and the same shall be done in thy case, which he says forthwith: And thou forgavest the wickedness of my heart.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wouldest thou see the loving-kindness of God, O thou that art lately come to the catechising?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wouldest thou see the loving-kindness of God, and the abundance of His long-suffering?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear about Adam.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Adam, God’s first-formed man, transgressed: could He not at once have brought death upon him?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But see what the Lord does, in His great love towards man.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He casts him out from Paradise, for because of sin he was unworthy to live there; but He puts him to dwell over against Paradise: that seeing whence he had fallen, and from what and into what a state he was brought down, he might afterwards be saved by repentance.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Cain the first-born man became his brother’s murderer, the inventor of evils, the first author of murders, and the first envious man.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Yet after slaying his brother to what is he condemned?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Groaning and trembling shalt thou be upon the earth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: How great the offence, the sentence how light!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Even this then was truly loving-kindness in God, but little as yet in comparison with what follows.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For consider what happened in the days of Noe.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The giants sinned, and much wickedness was then spread over the earth, and because of this the flood was to come upon them: and in the five hundredth year God utters His threatening; but in the six hundredth He brought the flood upon the earth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Seest thou the breadth of God’s loving-kindness extending to a hundred years?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Could He not have done immediately what He did then after the hundred years?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But He extended (the time) on purpose, granting a respite for repentance.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Seest thou God’s goodness?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And if the men of that time had repented, they would not have missed the loving-kindness of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Come with me now to the other class, those who were saved by repentance.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But perhaps even among women some one will say, I have committed fornication, and adultery, I have defiled my body by excesses of all kinds: is there salvation for me?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Turn thine eyes, O woman, upon Rahab, and look thou also for salvation; for if she who had been openly and publicly a harlot was saved by repentance, is not she who on some one occasion before receiving grace committed fornication to be saved by repentance and fasting?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For inquire how she was saved: this only she said: For your God is God in heaven and upon earth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Your God; for her own she did not dare to say, because of her wanton life.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And if you wish to receive Scriptural testimony of her having been saved, you have it written in the Psalms: I will make mention of Rahab and Babylon among them that know me.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: O the greatness of God’s loving-kindness, making mention even of harlots in the Scriptures: nay, not simply I will make mention of Rahab and Babylon, but with the addition, among them that know me.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: There is then in the case both of men and of women alike the salvation which is ushered in by repentance.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nay more, if a whole people sin, this surpasses not the loving-kindness of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The people made a calf, yet God ceased not from His loving-kindness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Men denied God, but God denied not Himself.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: These be thy gods, O Israel, they said: yet again, as He was wont, the God of Israel became their Saviour.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And not only the people sinned, but also Aaron the High Priest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For it is Moses that says: And the anger of the Lord came upon Aaron: and I prayed for him, saith he, and God forgave him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What then, did Moses praying for a High Priest that sinned prevail with God, and shall not Jesus, His Only-begotten, prevail with God when He prays for us?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And if He did not hinder Aaron, because of his offence, from entering upon the High Priesthood, will He hinder thee, who art come out from the Gentiles, from entering into salvation?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Only, O man, repent thou also in like manner, and grace is not forbidden thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Render thy way of life henceforth unblameable; for God is truly loving unto man, nor can all time worthily tell out His loving kindness; nay, not if all the tongues of men unite together will they be able even so to declare any considerable part of His loving-kindness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For we tell some part of what is written concerning His loving-kindness to men, but how much He forgave the Angels we know not: for them also He forgives, since One alone is without sin, even Jesus who purgeth our sins.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And of them we have said enough.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But if concerning us men thou wilt have other examples also set before thee, come on to the blessed David, and take him for an example of repentance.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Great as he was, he fell: after his sleep, walking in the eventide on the housetop, he cast a careless look, and felt a human passion.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: His sin was completed, but there died not with it his candour concerning the confession of his fault.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nathan the Prophet came, a swift accuser, and a healer of the wound.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Lord is wroth, he says, and thou hast sinned.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: So spake the subject to the reigning king.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But David the king was not indignant, for he regarded not the speaker, but God who had sent him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He was not puffed up by the array of soldiers standing round: for he had seen in thought the angel-host of the Lord, and he trembled as seeing Him who is invisible; and to the messenger, or rather by him in answer to God who sent him, he said, I have sinned against the Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Seest thou the humility of the king?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Seest thou his confession?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For had he been convicted by any one?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Were many privy to the matter?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The deed was quickly done, and straightway the Prophet appeared as accuser, and the offender confesses the fault.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And because he candidly confessed, he received a most speedy cure.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For Nathan the Prophet who had uttered the threat, said immediately, The Lord also hath put away thy sin.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest the swift relenting of a merciful God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He says, however, Thou hast greatly provoked the enemies of the Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Though thou hadst many enemies because of thy righteousness, thy self-control protected thee; but now that thou hast surrendered thy strongest armour, thine enemies are risen up, and stand ready against thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thus then did the Prophet comfort him, but the blessed David, for all he heard it said, The Lord hath put away thy sin, did not cease from repentance, king though he was, but put on sackcloth instead of purple, and instead of a golden throne, he sat, a king, in ashes on the ground; nay, not only sat in ashes, but also had ashes for his food, even as he saith himself, I have eaten ashes as it were bread.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: His lustful eye he wasted away with tears saying, Every night will I wash my couch, and water my bed with my tears.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When his officers besought him to eat bread he would not listen.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He prolonged his fast unto seven whole days.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If a king thus made confession oughtest not thou, a private person, to confess?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Again, after Absalom’s insurrection, though there were many roads for him to escape, he chose to flee by the Mount of Olives, in thought, as it were, invoking the Redeemer who was to go up thence into the heavens.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And when Shimei cursed him bitterly, he said, Let him alone, for he knew that “to him that forgiveth it shall be forgiven.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest that it is good to make confession.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest that there is salvation for them that repent.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Solomon also fell but what saith he?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Afterwards I repented.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Ahab, too, the King of Samaria, became a most wicked idolater, an outrageous man, the murderer of the Prophets, a stranger to godliness, a coveter of other men’s fields and vineyards.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Yet when by Jezebel’s means he had slain Naboth, and the Prophet Elias came and merely threatened him, he rent his garments, and put on sackcloth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And what saith the merciful God to Elias?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hast than seen how Ahab is pricked in the heart before Me? as if almost He would persuade the fiery zeal of the Prophet to condescend to the penitent.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He saith, I will not bring the evil in his days.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And though after this forgiveness he was sure not to depart from his wickedness, nevertheless the forgiving God forgave him, not as being ignorant of the future, but as granting a forgiveness corresponding to his present season of repentance.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For it is the part of a righteous judge to give sentence according to each case that has occurred.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Again, Jeroboam was standing at the altar sacrificing to the idols: his hand became withered, because he commanded the Prophet who reproved him to be seized: but having by experience learned the power of the man before him, he says, Entreat the face of the Lord thy God; and because of this saying his hand was restored again.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If the Prophet healed Jeroboam, is Christ not able to heal and deliver thee from thy sins?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Manasses also was utterly wicked, who sawed Isaiah asunder, and was defiled with all kinds of idolatries, and filled Jerusalem with innocent blood; but having been led captive to Babylon he used his experience of misfortune for a healing course of repentance: for the Scripture saith that Manasses humbled himself before the Lord, and prayed, and the Lord heard him, and brought him back to his kingdom.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If He who sawed the Prophet asunder was saved by repentance, shall not thou then, having done no such great wickedness, be saved?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Take heed lest without reason thou mistrust the power of repentance.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wouldst thou know what power repentance has?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wouldst thou know the strong weapon of salvation, and learn what the force of confession is?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hezekiah by means of confession routed a hundred and fourscore and five thousand of his enemies.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A great thing verily was this, but still small in comparison with what remains to be told: the same king by repentance obtained the recall of a divine sentence which had already gone forth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For when he had fallen sick, Esaias said to him, Set thine house in order; for thou shalt die, and not live.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What expectation remained, what hope of recovery, when the Prophet said, for thou shalt die?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Yet Hezekiah did not desist from repentance; but remembering what is written, When thou shalt turn and lament, then shalt thou be saved, he turned to the wall, and from his bed lifting his mind to heaven (for thickness of walls is no hindrance to prayers sent up with devotion), he said, “Remember me, O Lord, for it is sufficient for my healing that Thou remember me.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou art not subject to times, but art Thyself the giver of the law of life.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For our life depends not on a nativity, nor on a conjunction of stars, as some idly talk; but both of life and its duration.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then art Thyself the Lawgiver according to Thy Will.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And he, who could not hope to live because of the prophetic sentence, had fifteen years added to his life, and for the sign the sun ran backward in his course.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Well then, for Ezekias’ sake the sun turned back but for Christ the sun was eclipsed, not retracing his steps, but suffering eclipse, and therefore shewing the difference between them, I mean between Ezekias and Jesus.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The former prevailed to the cancelling of God’s decree, and cannot Jesus grant remission of sins?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Turn and bewail thyself, shut thy door, and pray to be forgiven, pray that He may remove from thee the burning flames.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For confession has power to quench even fire, power to tame even lions.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But if thou disbelieve, consider what befel Ananias and his companions.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What streams did they pour out?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: How many vessels of water could quench the flame that rose up forty-nine cubits high?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nay, but where the flame mounted up a little too high, faith was there poured out as a river, and there spake they the spell against all ills: Righteous art Thou, O Lord, in all the things that Thou hast done to us: for we have sinned, and transgressed Thy law.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And their repentance quelled the flames.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If thou believest not that repentance is able to quench the fire of hell, learn it from what happened in regard to Ananias.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But some keen hearer will say, Those men God rescued justly in that case: because they refused to commit idolatry, God gave them that power.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And since this thought has occurred, I come next to a different example of penitence.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What thinkest thou of Nabuchodonosor?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hast thou not heard out of the Scriptures that he was bloodthirsty, fierce, lion-like in disposition?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hast thou not heard that he brought out the bones of the kings from their graves into the light?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hast thou not heard that he carried the people away captive?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hast thou not heard that he put out the eyes of the king, after he had already seen his children slain?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hast thou not heard that he brake in pieces the Cherubim?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I do not mean the invisible beings;—away with such a thought, O man,—but the sculptured images, and the mercy-seat, in the midst of which God spake with His voice.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The veil of the Sanctuary he trampled under foot: the altar of incense he took and carried away to an idol-temple: all the offerings he took away: the Temple he burned from the foundations.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: How great punishments did he deserve, for slaying kings, for setting fire to the Sanctuary, for taking the people captive, for setting the sacred vessels in the house of idols?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Did he not deserve ten thousand deaths?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast seen the greatness of his evil deeds: come now to God’s loving-kindness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He was turned into a wild beast, he abode in the wilderness, he was scourged, that he might be saved.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He had claws as a lion; for he was a ravager of the Sanctuary.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He had a lion’s mane: for he was a ravening and a roaring lion.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He ate grass like an ox: for a brute beast he was, not knowing Him who had given him the kingdom.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: His body was wet from the dew; because after seeing the fire quenched by the dew he believed not.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And what happened?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: After this, saith he, I, Nabuchodonosor, lifted up mine eyes unto heaven, and I blessed the Most High, and to Him that liveth for ever I gave praise and glory.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When, therefore, he recognised the Most High, and sent up these words of thankfulness to God, and repented himself for what he had done, and recognised his own weakness, then God gave back to him the honour of the kingdom.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When Nabuchodonosor, after having done such deeds, had made confession, did God give him pardon and the kingdom, and when thou repentest shall He not give thee the remission of sins, and the kingdom of heaven, if thou live a worthy life?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Lord is loving unto man, and swift to pardon, but slow to punish.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let no man therefore despair of his own salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Peter, the chiefest and foremost of the Apostles, denied the Lord thrice before a little maid: but he repented himself, and wept bitterly.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now weeping shews the repentance of the heart: and therefore he not only received forgiveness for his denial, but also held his Apostolic dignity unforfeited.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Having therefore, brethren, many examples of those who have sinned and repented and been saved, do ye also heartily make confession unto the Lord, that ye may both receive the forgiveness of your former sins, and be counted worthy of the heavenly gift, and inherit the heavenly kingdom with all the saints in Christ Jesus; to Whom is the glory for ever and ever.
